title: School of Medical and Allied Sciences | Galgotias University
content: Funded Projects Publication WOS/Scopus Student Publication Patent Published/Granted Centre of Excellence The School of Allied Health Sciences SAHS at Galgotias University is renowned for its commitment to excellence in education and training in various healthcare disciplines. Within this esteemed institution, the departments cater to diverse fields, including Physiotherapy, Cardiovascular Therapy, Lab Sciences, and Optometry. Let's reimagine the provided passages accordingly: Physiotherapy and Allied Health Sciences Department: Galgotias University's School of Allied Health Sciences boasts a vibrant and pioneering department dedicated to Physiotherapy and Allied Health Sciences. Our mission is to cultivate proficient professionals equipped with the requisite knowledge and skills to address the evolving demands of the healthcare sector. By offering cutting-edge facilities Electrotherapy labs, OPD, Estimed faculty, and a robust curriculum, the department endeavors to foster an environment conducive to academic distinction, intellectual inquisitiveness, and analytical acumen. We are committed to nurturing future leaders devoted to enhancing the health and well-being of individuals and communities through evidence-based practices and compassionate care. Cardiovascular Therapy Department: At Galgotias University's School of Allied Health Sciences, our Cardiovascular Therapy Department stands as a beacon of excellence in cardiovascular care education. Our unwavering commitment is to empower students to become adept professionals poised to excel in the dynamic landscape of cardiovascular healthcare. Through state-of-the-art facilities and a rigorous curriculum, we strive to instill a passion for innovation and critical thinking in our students. By providing comprehensive theoretical knowledge and hands-on clinical experience, we aim to produce graduates who are well-prepared to address the complex challenges of cardiovascular therapy with confidence and competence. Laboratory Sciences Department: The Laboratory Sciences Department at Galgotias University's School of Allied Health Sciences is at the forefront of advancing education and training in laboratory sciences. Our primary objective is to nurture raw talent and mold individuals into proficient practitioners who demonstrate excellence in their respective fields. With a steadfast commitment to enhancing teaching methodologies and skill development, we aim to meet the escalating demand for skilled laboratory professionals in the healthcare industry. Through immersive laboratory training and clinical exposure, we endeavor to hone the competencies of our students and provide them with the tools for a rewarding career in laboratory sciences. Optometry Department: Galgotias University's School of Allied Health Sciences takes pride in its Optometry Department, renowned for its dedication to providing premier education and training in optometry. Our comprehensive programs are meticulously designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary to thrive in the field of optometry. With a focus on fostering a culture of innovation and critical thinking, we prepare our students to embrace emerging technologies and advancements in eye care. Through a blend of theoretical instruction and hands-on clinical experience, we aspire to produce optometry professionals who are proficient, compassionate, and poised to make meaningful contributions to eye health and vision care. Medical Radiology and Imaging Technology To be a leading program in Medical Radiology and Imaging Technology, renowned for producing highly skilled, compassionate, and innovative radiologic technologists who excel in patient care, technological proficiency, and professional leadership in the ever-evolving field of medical imaging. Program: B.Pharm Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business UP Government University Grants Commission All India Council for Technical Education The Bar Council of India Indian Nursing Council National Council for Teacher Education.
